new_classes.definition_en: Water that complies with Directive 2009/54/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council on the exploitation and marketing of natural mineral waters, presented in a container of 2 litres or more (CN 2201 10 19).

A quality borne by a water product indicating that it fulfils all requirements of Directive 2009/54/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council on the exploitation and marketing of natural mineral waters.

coverage_explanation: The two primary distinguishing criteria for CN 2201 10 19 are: (1) the water must qualify as 'natural mineral water' under Directive 2009/54/EC — captured by the Directive200954ECCompliance quality restriction; and (2) the container size must be ≥ 2 L (the '19' subcode distinguishes large containers from the '11' subcode for containers < 2 L) — captured by the LargeContainer parthood restriction. Coverage is not 1.0 because the internal content of Directive 2009/54/EC (specific physicochemical criteria, microbiological purity, geological origin, stability of composition, etc.) cannot be fully enumerated as OWL axioms without a dedicated extension ontology for regulatory compliance attributes. The container-size cut-off is modelled via the existing eucn:LargeContainer class (≥ 2 L), which aligns with the split between 2201 10 11 (< 2 L) and 2201 10 19 (≥ 2 L).
